caption: This video captures a technical presentation on "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" for "Tutorial – 4," as indicated by the headers. The screen displays mathematical calculations for "Work/unit mass of gas liquefied" and the "Figure of Merit (FOM)." Initial equations show $-\frac{W}{m} = 486.2$ and $y = 0.0215$, leading to a calculated value of $22613.95\text{ J/g}$. As the sequence progresses, the "Figure of Merit (FOM)" section is elaborated upon. The text $-\frac{W}{m_f} = 22613.95$ and $-\frac{W}{m_f} = 761$ appear, culminating in a final calculation where $\text{FOM} = \frac{W_c}{m_f} = \frac{761}{22613.95} = 0.0336$. The bottom of the screen credits "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay" and features an "NPTEL" logo in the bottom-left corner. A red circular cursor moves across the screen, highlighting the evolving calculations and the final result.
